clusters.headline: South Africa: NGO Demands Fast Sentencing for Child Abuser - South African News Briefs - July 16, 2026
Special police team to investigate killing of an anti-migrant leader in South Africa
clusters.summary: The March and March provincial leader died of his injuries in hospital several days after being shot.

clusters.headline: Rastafarians in Kenya fail in bid to get cannabis legalised for religious purposes
clusters.summary: The court acknowledged the need for a broader national debate on cannabis policy in Kenya.

clusters.headline: Kenya: Court rejects Rastafari bid for religious freedom marijuana exemption
clusters.summary: Kenya's small Rastafarian community was seeking permission to smoke marijuana under the constitutional right to freedom of religion. The judge found that they did not show it to be a necessary part of their worship.

clusters.headline: Pete Hegseth says US military to start testosterone screening for over 30s
clusters.summary: US Defense Secretary says plan is 'about restoring and optimising ... natural capabilities' of troops.

clusters.headline: New monkey species with orange lips found 'hiding' in DR Congo forest
clusters.summary: Scientists describe "amazing feeling" to look into the face of an animal that so few people knew existed.

clusters.headline: Eleven people die in fire at Algerian foster care home
clusters.summary: Along with the fatalities, 19 people suffered injuries, the authorities say.

clusters.headline: Kenya: Kenya's 'Political Gangs' Fuel Election Fears
clusters.summary: [DW] Violent disruptions of political and civic events are raising concerns about the use of hired gangs as Kenya heads toward elections in 2027.

clusters.headline: Ten years ago, parts of the Turkish military tried to overthrow the Erdogan government. The consequences are still shaping Turkey today
clusters.summary: Ten years ago, parts of the Turkish military attempted to overthrow the government of President Recep Tayyip Erdogan. The consequences of the failed coup continue to shape Turkey to this day. DW looks back.

clusters.headline: Congo-Kinshasa: 'In the DR Congo, Containing Ebola Requires Stronger Collective Action and a Faster Response'
clusters.summary: [Unicef] Dakar -- Statement by UNICEF Regional Director for West and Central Africa, Gilles Fagninou, following visit to Ituri

clusters.headline: Singaporean traveller stranded at Malaysia rest stop at 4.15am after coach to KL drove off without him
clusters.summary: Even before the incident, the trip had already been marred by a last-minute schedule change and delay.

clusters.headline: Malawi: Alarm As Over 100 Underage Girls Trapped in Sex Work in Rumphi, Campaigners War
clusters.summary: [Nyasa Times] More than 100 girls under the age of 18 are believed to be involved in sex work in Rumphi, according to warnings issued by campaigners at a stakeholders' meeting this week.
